description: REQ-EDIT-005 / REQ-EDIT-020 / REQ-EDIT-032 / PROP-EDIT-006 require that the Copy button is enabled when `status ∈ {editing, saving} && !isNoteEmpty` regardless of `isDirty`. The pure predicate `canCopy` is correct (editorPredicates.ts:18-33). The shell additionally narrows enable to `&& !viewState.isDirty`, which is not in the spec and contradicts the explicit REQ-EDIT-020 acceptance criterion 'The Copy button is enabled when isNoteEmpty === false'. This makes the Copy button unusable during the entire dirty editing period that any normal user spends typing — the default state in which a user would actually want to copy. No DOM test currently exercises copy-while-dirty (editor-session-state.dom.vitest.ts line 152 sets `isDirty: false` for the enabled assertion), so the bug slips through.
suggestedRemediation: Change to `const isCopyEnabled = $derived(canCopy(viewState));` and remove the misleading inline comment that fabricates a non-existent 'editing+dirty' disable rule. Add a regression test: editing + isDirty=true + isNoteEmpty=false → copy-body-button has no disabled attribute and click dispatches CopyNoteBody.
